events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes flex duct with visible sags between hangers and joints sealed only with gray cloth duct tape (no mastic, UL-listed foil tape, or mechanical draw bands). This is non-compliant per IMC 603, SMACNA, and manufacturer instructions. Flex duct must be supported at intervals (typically every 4 feet max per manufacturer), sag limited, and joints must be sealed with approved methods (mastic, UL-listed tape, mechanical fasteners). Gray cloth duct tape is not an approved sealant for duct joints.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium (energy loss, poor performance, not immediate safety hazard but code violation)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 90 (rough-complete)
Confidence: 0.95
Findings: flex sag between hangers, unsealed joints using gray duct tape only, no mastic/UL foil tape/draw bands
Actions: re-support flex duct to manufacturer spacing, remove tape, seal joints with mastic or UL-listed foil tape and mechanical draw bands
References: IMC 603, SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, Manual D, manufacturer installation instructions

    "Cold room complaint consistent with leakage and reduced effective airflow from sagged/unsealed branch"

  "actions": [
    "Re-support flex duct at manufacturer-required intervals (typically 4 ft max) with no sag exceeding 1/2 in per foot",
    "Remove gray cloth duct tape from all metal-to-flex joints",
    "Seal joints with mastic or UL-listed foil tape per IMC 603 and manufacturer instructions",
    "Install mechanical draw bands/clamps at all flex-to-metal connections and verify alignment",
    "Test/verify airflow and static pressure after corrections"
  ],
  "rationale": "Flex duct must be supported per manufacturer and SMACNA limits to prevent sag, and duct joints must be sealed with approved sealants and mechanical fasteners. Gray cloth duct tape is not an approved sealing method under IMC 603 or manufacturer instructions, and visible sag indicates non-compliant support spacing.",

    "IMC 603 (Duct Construction and Installation)",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards / Flex Duct Installation Guidelines",

    "Flexible duct manufacturer installation instructions"
  ]
